headline: US and Iran trade strikes, threatening fragile truce
description: Iran's central military command accused the US of violating the ceasefire with attacks on vessels in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday, saying they struck US forces in retaliation. The US, however, says Iran attacked three American destroyers first, which Trump later dismissed as a 'trifle,' and said he considered the ceasefire still active.

headline: 'Faced with Trump and Putin, the war in Ukraine is acting as a catalyst for Europe's autonomization'
From the 'Andes strain' to the 'fatality rate,' a glossary of terms for understanding hantaviruses
Welsh nationalists poised for historic election breakthrough
description: For the first time in its history, the pro-independence Welsh party Plaid Cymru could top the Welsh Parliament in Thursday's election. The surge reflects not just disillusionment with rivals but a deeper frustration at years of being sidelined by London.

headline: Trump and Brazil's Lula hail positive talks after rocky relations
description: The three-hour meeting came as a boost for Brazilian President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva, who is seeking a fourth, non-consecutive term in office – and is facing a tight election in October against Flavio Bolsonaro, the son of former far-right president Jair Bolsonaro, a close Trump ally.

headline: After PSG qualifies for the Champions League final, fans face travel headaches getting to Budapest
description: Paris Saint-Germain supporters hoping to attend the match against Arsenal on May 30 in the Hungarian capital must face high prices and the club's very selective ticket allocation process.

headline: Russia, Israel, United States: 'Pariah' pavilions spark controversy at Venice Biennale
description: The major international art event, which this year reopened its doors to Russia, is facing widespread protests. On the ground, artists are divided.

Scottish elections: Reform UK capitalizes on anti-migrant protests in Falkirk
description: As regional elections take place on Thursday, May 7, in Scotland and Wales, far-right activists have managed to rally broad support around slogans hostile to asylum seekers in a region previously known for being welcoming.

headline: Military personnel at French presidency suspected of compromising classified documents
description: Prosecutors are investigating several members of the military and civilians suspected of setting up a private company to sell classified information and weapons of war. Six suspects have been charged.

headline: Jean Pisani-Ferry: 'The United States has lost its moral authority for good'
description: The United States is turning its back on democracy and freedom – values the nation once embodied – and is now replacing them with the law of the strongest and a culture of deal-making. Donald Trump has caused irreparable harm to the world's leading power, argues the economist in his column.
